summary:
The agent correctly identifies that topical steroids are most effective in eczematous dermatitis due to their proven ability to suppress inflammation and alleviate symptoms without promoting infection or causing harm during acute phases. In contrast, other options either represent harmful effects (like dermal atrophy) or situations where steroids exacerbate pathology rather than improve outcomes—in particular, active herpetic infections—which makes those indications inappropriate despite occasional off-label uses.

The correct answer choice reflecting optimal therapeutic benefit and established efficacy is therefore $\boxed{\text{B}}$.
